question_text: A student builds a roller coaster with a loop. At the highest point of the loop, the cart has gravitational potential energy of 5000 J and kinetic energy of 1000 J. At the lowest point of the loop, the gravitational potential energy is 0 J. Assuming no energy is lost to friction, calculate the kinetic energy at the lowest point.
explanation: Total energy at the top = 5000 J + 1000 J = 6000 J. At the lowest point, all potential energy converts to kinetic energy (since potential energy is now 0 J). By conservation of energy, kinetic energy at the bottom = 6000 J.
